---
title: "Pushing the AR test beyond the limit"
subtitle: "A novel goodness-of-fit test for high-dimensional applications"
author: "Markku Kuismin"
institute: "University of Oulu, Northern Finland Birth Cohorts"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    lib_dir: libs
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
---

```{r out.width='100%', fig.height=6, eval=require('leaflet'), echo=FALSE}
library(leaflet)
leaflet() %>%
  addTiles() %>%
  setView(25.23685, 62.58855,  zoom = 4.5) %>%
    addMarkers(c(25.5242, 24.9495), c(65.0075, 60.1696), label = c("25.52, 65.01", "60.17, 24.95"))
```

---

class: center, middle

# Background and motivation

---

# Background and motivation

- In several applications, we want to know if random variable $X$ follows a specific multivariate distribution.

--

- For example, many methods for high-dimensional data (e.g., Gaussian Graphical Models) assume the population is normal.

---

# Background and motivation

- Until we have a test that can detect the difference in 100\% power in every single scenario, this is still an open research problem...

---

# The A-R statistic

---

- 

--

-

---

class: center, middle

# Thanks!

Slides created via the R package [**xaringan**](https://github.com/yihui/xaringan).

(Something a bit different for Beamer presentations.)
